REFERENCE: The reference is largely authentic but contains a significant formatting error in the volume/issue/pages section. The authors, title, journal, year (2022), and DOI (10.1056/NEJMoa2116846) are all real and verifiable correspond to the PINETREE trial published in the New England Journal of Medicine. The page numbers (305-315) are correct. However, the citation lists only '(4)' where the volume number should be. The actual volume is 386 (Issue 4). The format should be: '386(4), 305-315' rather than '(4), 305-315'. This is a common omission or formatting mistake when copying citations.
authors: The authors listed (Gottlieb, Vaca, Paredes, Mera, Webb, Perez, Oguchi, Ryan, Nielsen, Brown, Hidalgo, Sachdeva, Mittal, Osiyemi, Skarbinski, Juneja, Hyland, Osinusi, Chen, Camus, and the GS-US-540-9012 (PINETREE) Investigators) are consistent with the published PINETREE trial. The first author Robert L. Gottlieb and the trial group are correctly attributed.
title: The title 'Early Remdesivir to Prevent Progression to Severe Covid-19 in Outpatients' is correct and matches the published article exactly.
journal_name: The journal name is correct: The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM). The full title is present, though formatting conventions typically omit the initial article 'The' or abbreviate the name.
journal_volume: The volume number is listed incorrectly or is missing. The citation shows '(4), 305-315', where '(4)' refers to the issue number, not the volume. The correct volume is 386. The format should be '386(4), 305-315'. This is an error in the citation formatting.
journal_issue: The journal name 'The New England journal of medicine' is correct (though conventionally formatted as 'New England Journal of Medicine' or abbreviated 'N Engl J Med' in most citation styles). The issue number (4) is correct for this article, which appeared in Volume 386, Issue 4, February 3, 2022.
journal_page: The pages 305-315 are correct for the PDF/print version of the article. This is consistent with the published record.
elocator: The elocator/formatting is incomplete. The citation shows '(4), 305-315', but the correct format should include the volume number. The actual citation is: N Engl J Med. 2022;386(4):305-315. The volume number 386 is missing, and the issue number (4) is presented incorrectly as if it were the volume. This is a significant formatting error that could cause confusion.
publication_date: The publication date of 2022 is correct for this PINETREE study. It was published online around January 2022 and appeared in the print issue on February 3, 2022.
doi: The DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2116846 is a valid DOI that resolves to the correct article in the New England Journal of Medicine: 'Early Remdesivir to Prevent Progression to Severe Covid-19 in Outpatients' by Gottlieb et al.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in this citation. Given that this is a New England Journal of Medicine article, it is typically available in PubMed Central (PMC). REFERENCE: This is a verified, legitimate reference. The PINETREE trial (NCT04501952) was a landmark randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial demonstrating that early 3-day remdesivir treatment reduced progression to severe COVID-19 in high-risk outpatients. All components of the citation are accurate.
authors: The author list includes numerous real researchers who were involved in the PINETREE trial. The first author Gottlieb is indeed Robert L. Gottlieb who led this trial. The inclusion of 'GS-US-540-9012 (PINETREE) Investigators' as a group author is consistent with how clinical trials typically list collaborative authorship. The names match those published in the actual NEJM paper.
title: The title 'Early Remdesivir to Prevent Progression to Severe Covid-19 in Outpatients' matches the actual published title of the PINETREE trial in NEJM. The title accurately describes the study which evaluated remdesivir use in outpatient settings.
journal_name: The New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) is a real, highly prestigious medical journal. This study was indeed published there.
journal_volume: The citation does not explicitly state a volume number but provides issue/page information. The actual paper was published in NEJM volume 386, issue 4. journal_issue: The issue number (4) matches the actual publication. The PINETREE trial was published in NEJM 2022;386(4):305-315.
journal_page: The page numbers 305-315 match the actual publication of this study in NEJM.
elocator: No elocator is provided. The DOI is given separately. The NEJM article does have a DOI of 10.1056/NEJMoa2116846 which matches what's provided.
publication_date: The year 2022 is correct for the PINETREE trial publication. The actual publication date was January 27, 2022 (online ahead of print), with print publication in the February 3, 2022 issue (volume 386, issue 4).
doi: The DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2116846 is the correct DOI for this article. It can be verified at doi.org and resolves to the NEJM article.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the citation.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the citation, though the article has PMID 34937145 which could be added.
